top_signals.title: Will China and the United States Pursue Strategic Stability?
top_signals.summary:
In 2024, Michael Swaine wrote, “How to Stop the United States and China from Sliding into War,” where he identified areas that could increase the possibility of an armed conflict between the United States and China. Two years later, after recent talks between President Trump and President Xi, we asked Michael to revisit his arguments.Image: U.S. Department of StateIn your 2024 article, you flagged a rising possibility of major armed conflict between China and the United States. That was before American forces became militarily involved in Iran. Does that involvement change your calculus? And critically — does it deter Beijing,

The post Will China and the United States Pursue Strategic Stability? appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China, HK Investors Banned From SpaceX IPO on Security Grounds
top_signals.summary: Underwriters on SpaceX’s $75 billion initial public offering have been told not to accept orders from investors in Hong Kong and China, citing US restrictions around the export of critical technology, people with knowledge of the matter said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Where investors may find the next 'big wave' for AI trade
top_signals.summary: Tim Urbanowicz, chief investment strategist at Innovator from Goldman Sachs Asset Management, tackles the AI boom.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin caps a dismal week as price wallows 50% below its all-time high
top_signals.summary: A rebound in the digital currency, or characteristic dip-buying by investors, is yet to take place.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Natwest to pump £50m into branches after shuttering over a thousand
top_signals.summary: Natwest is planning to beef up its branch network after being one of the leading banks to take the axe to its physical presence over the last decade.  The blue-chip banking giant will pump £50m into its network over the next 18 months and has pledged not to close any branches until at least 2029. [...]

top_signals.title: Hapag-Lloyd and Seaspan Complete First Methanol Retrofit
top_signals.summary: Hapag-Lloyd and Seaspan Corporation have completed the first conversion under their joint methanol retrofit program.With delivery of the Seaspan Yangtze, the first of five 10,100 TEU charter vessels has been upgraded from a conventional MAN…
